UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile within the Nexus Alumni system, whether a student or a professor, linking to an external authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

externalAuthId.description: Identifier from the external authentication system (e.g., Firebase Auth UID). This ID establishes the link between an authenticated user and their profile data. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 ExternalAuthUser)

role.description: The type of user, indicating whether they are a 'Student' or 'Professor'. This determines which specific profile fields are applicable.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The primary email address of the user, used for contact and potentially as a unique identifier for external systems.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the user.

avatarUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture or avatar.

bio.description: A short biographical description or 'about me' section provided by the user.

linkedInUrl.description: URL to the user's LinkedIn profile for professional networking.

major.description: The academic major pursued by the student (applicable if role is 'Student').

graduationYear.description: The year the student graduated or expects to graduate (applicable if role is 'Student').

department.description: The academic department the professor is affiliated with (applicable if role is 'Professor').

researchInterests.description: A list of research areas or topics the professor is interested in or specializes in (applicable if role is 'Professor').

interests.description: General personal or professional interests of the user, used for recommendations and connections.

careerGoals.description: Career aspirations and goals of the user, used for mentorship matching and job recommendations.

isVisibleInDirectory.description: Indicates whether the user's profile is publicly visible in the alumni directory.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated in the system.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents an alumni event, such as a reunion, career fair, or networking opportunity, displayed in the events calendar.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

title.description: The title or name of the event.

description.description: A detailed description of the event, including agenda, speakers, and other relevant information.

location.description: The physical address or virtual meeting link where the event will take place.

startDateTime.description: The precise start date and time of the event.

endDateTime.description: The precise end date and time of the event.

organizerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the individual or organization responsible for organizing the event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Event)

registrationUrl.description: URL for external event registration, if applicable.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image, banner, or flyer associated with the event.

attendeeIds.description: An array of UserProfile IDs representing attendees who have registered for or are planning to attend the event. (Relationship: Event N:N UserProfile)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the event record was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the event record was last updated in the system.

JobPosting.title: JobPosting
JobPosting.description: Represents a job opportunity posted on the alumni job board, allowing users to search and apply for positions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobPosting entity.

title.description: The title or designation of the job opening.

description.description: A detailed description of the job, including responsibilities, duties, and company culture.

companyName.description: The name of the company offering the job opportunity.

location.description: The geographical location of the job (e.g., city, state, country, or 'Remote').

salaryRange.description: The estimated salary range for the position (e.g., '$60,000 - $80,000 P.A.' or 'Competitive').

requirements.description: A list of required skills, qualifications, or experience for the job.

applicationDeadline.description: The deadline for submitting job applications.

applicationUrl.description: The URL where candidates can apply for the job.

posterId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who posted this job opening. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N JobPosting)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the job posting was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the job posting was last updated in the system.

MentorshipConnection.title: MentorshipConnection
MentorshipConnection.description: Represents an established or requested mentorship connection between a mentor and a mentee.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MentorshipConnection entity.

mentorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile acting as the mentor in this connection. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MentorshipConnection)

menteeId.description: Reference to the UserProfile acting as the mentee in this connection. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MentorshipConnection)

status.description: The current status of the mentorship connection (e.g., 'Pending', 'Active', 'Completed', 'Rejected').

requestedAt.description: Timestamp when the mentorship connection was initially requested by the mentee.

acceptedAt.description: Timestamp when the mentorship request was accepted by the mentor.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the mentorship connection was officially marked as completed.

initialMessage.description: The initial message sent by the mentee when requesting the mentorship connection.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`{userId}`) for each profile corresponds directly to the user's Firebase Authentication UID (which is also the `externalAuthId` field), enabling path-based ownership. Includes denormalized `isVisibleInDirectory` for public directory access control and simplified list queries.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user profile, which is the Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

definition.description: Contains details about various alumni events. Read access is available to all authenticated users. Write access is restricted to the `organizerId` specified within the document. Includes denormalized `organizerId` for authorization independence.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier for an event.

definition.description: Stores job opportunities posted on the alumni job board. Read access is available to all authenticated users. Write access is restricted to the `posterId` specified within the document. Includes denormalized `posterId` for authorization independence.

params.name: jobPostingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a job posting.

definition.description: Manages mentorship connections between alumni. Read and write access is restricted to either the `mentorId` or `menteeId` explicitly defined within the document. Includes denormalized `mentorId` and `menteeId` for authorization independence.

params.name: connectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a mentorship connection.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure for the Nexus Alumni system prioritizes Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure secure, scalable, and debuggable security rules. Each top-level collection (`users`, `events`, `jobPostings`, `mentorshipConnections`) is designed with a homogeneous security posture, meaning all documents within a given collection share similar access patterns.

**Authorization Independence:** This is critically achieved through denormalization. Instead of relying on `get()` calls in security rules to fetch data from parent documents or related entities for authorization, all necessary authorization context is stored directly within the document being accessed. For instance:

*   **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** The user's Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`) is used as the document ID (`{userId}`). This inherently makes ownership checks (`request.auth.uid == userId`) authorization-independent and highly efficient.
*   **Events (`/events/{eventId}`):** The `organizerId` is stored directly within each `Event` document. Rules can check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.organizerId` for write permissions, eliminating the need to look up the organizer's profile.
*   **Job Postings (`/jobPostings/{jobPostingId}`):** Similarly, the `posterId` is denormalized into each `JobPosting` document, allowing for direct ownership checks.
*   **Mentorship Connections (`/mentorshipConnections/{connectionId}`):** Both `mentorId` and `menteeId` are included in the `MentorshipConnection` document, enabling rules to verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.mentorId || request.auth.uid == resource.data.menteeId` for read/write access.

This denormalization strategy significantly simplifies security rules, makes them more robust against data inconsistencies, and allows for atomic operations (like creating a document without needing to pre-check parent permissions).

**QAPs (Query as Authorization Predicate) Support:** The structure inherently supports secure `list` operations (queries) because access rules are applied consistently across all documents in a collection and leverage fields directly present in the queried documents.

*   **Alumni Directory:** The `/users` collection, in conjunction with the `isVisibleInDirectory` field on `UserProfile`, allows for public `list` queries of visible profiles. Security rules will permit reading specific fields only when `resource.data.isVisibleInDirectory == true`, effectively making `list` queries secure and efficient. Client-side queries would include `where('isVisibleInDirectory', '==', true)`. 
*   **Events Calendar & Job Board:** Both `/events` and `/jobPostings` are designed for broad read access for all authenticated users, facilitating easy `list` queries by date, location, or other criteria without complex authorization logic.
*   **Mentorship Program:** Users can efficiently query for their own mentorship connections using `where('mentorId', '==', request.auth.uid)` or `where('menteeId', '==', request.auth.uid)`, as these IDs are directly available in the documents.

Overall, the design adheres to the core principles, ensuring clarity of intent, reliable authorization, and optimal performance for typical alumni system operations.
